title: NodeSplittingOn
lean_code_with_comments:
/-- The "1-copy" encoding of a vertex `v : α` in the carrier
`α ⊕ ↑W` of a node-split graph:

* if `v ∈ W`: returns `Sum.inr ⟨v, hv⟩`, the fresh `W^1`
  intervention-input label for `v`;
* if `v ∉ W`: returns `Sum.inl v`, matching the LN's convention
  `v^1 := v` for vertices outside `W`.

The complementary "0-copy" encoding `v^0 := Sum.inl v` is plain
`Sum.inl` -- the *canonical observation copy*, identified with the
original vertex (cf. the LN comment "we ... make the
identification $W = W^0$" in def_3_11). No wrapper needed (see the
design notes above for the asymmetry).

Noncomputable because membership in a general `Set α` need not be
decidable: we use `Classical.propDecidable` via `open Classical`.
Used Prop-valued only, so the classical choice has no observable
cost downstream. -/
noncomputable def split1 (W : Set α) (v : α) : α ⊕ ↑W :=
  open Classical in
  if h : v ∈ W then Sum.inr ⟨v, h⟩ else Sum.inl v

/-- The *node-splitting* of the CDMG `G` with respect to a set
`W ⊆ G.V` of output nodes: the new CDMG `G_{spl(W)}` over the
carrier `α ⊕ ↑W` obtained by duplicating each `w ∈ W` into a
0-copy `Sum.inl w` (receiving incoming edges; identified with the
original `w` via the canonical `inl` embedding) and a 1-copy
`Sum.inr ⟨w, hw⟩` (fresh intervention-input label; sends outgoing
edges), with a fresh directed edge `w^0 → w^1` for each
`w ∈ W`. See `lecture-notes/lecture_notes/graphs.tex` definition
`def:G_node-splitting` (def 3.11 of the LN).

The identification direction `Sum.inl = 0-copy` (canonical) follows
the LN's own hints in def_3_11 and def_3_12 (and matches the
Richardson--Robins SWIG convention `X = X^o`); see the design
notes above.

The four `@[simp]` projection / membership lemmas
`nodeSplittingOn_J`, `nodeSplittingOn_V`, `mem_nodeSplittingOn_E`,
`mem_nodeSplittingOn_L` below characterise the four components of
the result and are the gateway for every downstream rewrite. -/
noncomputable def nodeSplittingOn (G : CDMG α) (W : Set α)
    (hW : W ⊆ G.V) : CDMG (α ⊕ ↑W) where
  J := Sum.inl '' G.J
  V := Sum.inl '' G.V ∪ Set.range (Sum.inr : ↑W → α ⊕ ↑W)
  disjoint_JV := by
    rw [Set.disjoint_left]
    rintro x ⟨j, hj, rfl⟩ hxV
    rcases hxV with ⟨v, hv, hjv⟩ | ⟨w, hjw⟩
    · cases Sum.inl_injective hjv
      exact Set.disjoint_left.mp G.disjoint_JV hj hv
    · exact nomatch hjw
  E := (fun p : α × α => (split1 W p.1, Sum.inl p.2)) '' G.E
     ∪ Set.range (fun w : ↑W => ((Sum.inl (w : α) : α ⊕ ↑W), Sum.inr w))
  E_subset := by
    rintro ⟨a, b⟩ h
    rcases h with ⟨⟨v₁, v₂⟩, hE, hab⟩ | ⟨w, hab⟩
    · -- piece 1: original edge (v₁, v₂) ∈ G.E, relabeled.
      simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hab
      obtain ⟨rfl, rfl⟩ := hab
      refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
      · -- a = split1 W v₁ in J_split ∪ V_split
        by_cases hv₁ : v₁ ∈ W
        · rw [split1_of_mem hv₁]
          exact Or.inr (Or.inr ⟨⟨v₁, hv₁⟩, rfl⟩)
        · rw [split1_of_not_mem hv₁]
          rcases (G.E_subset hE).1 with hJ | hV
          · exact Or.inl ⟨v₁, hJ, rfl⟩
          · exact Or.inr (Or.inl ⟨v₁, hV, rfl⟩)
      · -- b = Sum.inl v₂ in V_split
        exact Or.inl ⟨v₂, (G.E_subset hE).2, rfl⟩
    · -- piece 2: split edge (Sum.inl w, Sum.inr w) for w ∈ ↑W.
      simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hab
      obtain ⟨rfl, rfl⟩ := hab
      refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
      · -- a = Sum.inl w.val in V_split (uses hW : W ⊆ G.V)
        exact Or.inr (Or.inl ⟨(w : α), hW w.property, rfl⟩)
      · -- b = Sum.inr w in V_split
        exact Or.inr ⟨w, rfl⟩
  L := (fun p : α × α => ((Sum.inl p.1 : α ⊕ ↑W), Sum.inl p.2)) '' G.L
  L_subset := by
    rintro ⟨a, b⟩ ⟨⟨v₁, v₂⟩, hL, hab⟩
    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hab
    obtain ⟨rfl, rfl⟩ := hab
    obtain ⟨hv₁V, hv₂V⟩ := G.L_subset hL
    exact ⟨Or.inl ⟨v₁, hv₁V, rfl⟩, Or.inl ⟨v₂, hv₂V, rfl⟩⟩
  L_irrefl := by
    rintro a₁ a₂ ⟨⟨v₁, v₂⟩, hL, hab⟩
    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hab
    obtain ⟨rfl, rfl⟩ := hab
    intro heq
    exact G.L_irrefl hL (Sum.inl_injective heq)
  L_symm := by
    rintro a₁ a₂ ⟨⟨v₁, v₂⟩, hL, hab⟩
    refine ⟨(v₂, v₁), G.L_symm hL, ?_⟩
    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hab ⊢
    exact ⟨hab.2, hab.1⟩
  disjoint_EL := by
    rw [Set.disjoint_left]
    rintro p hE ⟨⟨u₁, u₂⟩, huL, rfl⟩
    -- p has been substituted by (Sum.inl u₁, Sum.inl u₂).
    rcases hE with ⟨⟨v₁, v₂⟩, hvE, hvb⟩ | ⟨w, hvb⟩
    · -- piece 1: (split1 W v₁, Sum.inl v₂) = (Sum.inl u₁, Sum.inl u₂).
      simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hvb
      obtain ⟨h1, h2⟩ := hvb
      -- h1 : split1 W v₁ = Sum.inl u₁ ; h2 : Sum.inl v₂ = Sum.inl u₂.
      obtain ⟨_, hv₁u₁⟩ := inl_eq_split1_iff.mp h1.symm
      have hv₂u₂ : v₂ = u₂ := Sum.inl_injective h2
      subst hv₁u₁
      subst hv₂u₂
      exact Set.disjoint_left.mp G.disjoint_EL hvE huL
    · -- piece 2: (Sum.inl w.val, Sum.inr w) = (Sum.inl u₁, Sum.inl u₂).
      -- Target Sum.inr cannot equal Sum.inl -- immediate contradiction.
      simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hvb
      exact nomatch hvb.2
lean_code_without_comments:
noncomputable def split1 (W : Set α) (v : α) : α ⊕ ↑W :=
  open Classical in
  if h : v ∈ W then Sum.inr ⟨v, h⟩ else Sum.inl v

noncomputable def nodeSplittingOn (G : CDMG α) (W : Set α)
    (hW : W ⊆ G.V) : CDMG (α ⊕ ↑W) where
  J := Sum.inl '' G.J
  V := Sum.inl '' G.V ∪ Set.range (Sum.inr : ↑W → α ⊕ ↑W)
  disjoint_JV := by
    rw [Set.disjoint_left]
    rintro x ⟨j, hj, rfl⟩ hxV
    rcases hxV with ⟨v, hv, hjv⟩ | ⟨w, hjw⟩
    · cases Sum.inl_injective hjv
      exact Set.disjoint_left.mp G.disjoint_JV hj hv
    · exact nomatch hjw
  E := (fun p : α × α => (split1 W p.1, Sum.inl p.2)) '' G.E
     ∪ Set.range (fun w : ↑W => ((Sum.inl (w : α) : α ⊕ ↑W), Sum.inr w))
  E_subset := by
    rintro ⟨a, b⟩ h
    rcases h with ⟨⟨v₁, v₂⟩, hE, hab⟩ | ⟨w, hab⟩
    ·
      simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hab
      obtain ⟨rfl, rfl⟩ := hab
      refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
      ·
        by_cases hv₁ : v₁ ∈ W
        · rw [split1_of_mem hv₁]
          exact Or.inr (Or.inr ⟨⟨v₁, hv₁⟩, rfl⟩)
        · rw [split1_of_not_mem hv₁]
          rcases (G.E_subset hE).1 with hJ | hV
          · exact Or.inl ⟨v₁, hJ, rfl⟩
          · exact Or.inr (Or.inl ⟨v₁, hV, rfl⟩)
      ·
        exact Or.inl ⟨v₂, (G.E_subset hE).2, rfl⟩
    ·
      simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hab
      obtain ⟨rfl, rfl⟩ := hab
      refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
      ·
        exact Or.inr (Or.inl ⟨(w : α), hW w.property, rfl⟩)
      ·
        exact Or.inr ⟨w, rfl⟩
  L := (fun p : α × α => ((Sum.inl p.1 : α ⊕ ↑W), Sum.inl p.2)) '' G.L
  L_subset := by
    rintro ⟨a, b⟩ ⟨⟨v₁, v₂⟩, hL, hab⟩
    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hab
    obtain ⟨rfl, rfl⟩ := hab
    obtain ⟨hv₁V, hv₂V⟩ := G.L_subset hL
    exact ⟨Or.inl ⟨v₁, hv₁V, rfl⟩, Or.inl ⟨v₂, hv₂V, rfl⟩⟩
  L_irrefl := by
    rintro a₁ a₂ ⟨⟨v₁, v₂⟩, hL, hab⟩
    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hab
    obtain ⟨rfl, rfl⟩ := hab
    intro heq
    exact G.L_irrefl hL (Sum.inl_injective heq)
  L_symm := by
    rintro a₁ a₂ ⟨⟨v₁, v₂⟩, hL, hab⟩
    refine ⟨(v₂, v₁), G.L_symm hL, ?_⟩
    simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hab ⊢
    exact ⟨hab.2, hab.1⟩
  disjoint_EL := by
    rw [Set.disjoint_left]
    rintro p hE ⟨⟨u₁, u₂⟩, huL, rfl⟩

    rcases hE with ⟨⟨v₁, v₂⟩, hvE, hvb⟩ | ⟨w, hvb⟩
    ·
      simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hvb
      obtain ⟨h1, h2⟩ := hvb

      obtain ⟨_, hv₁u₁⟩ := inl_eq_split1_iff.mp h1.symm
      have hv₂u₂ : v₂ = u₂ := Sum.inl_injective h2
      subst hv₁u₁
      subst hv₂u₂
      exact Set.disjoint_left.mp G.disjoint_EL hvE huL
    ·

      simp only [Prod.mk.injEq] at hvb
      exact nomatch hvb.2
lean_explanation:
The Lean definition takes three arguments: a CDMG `G : CDMG α` over an ambient vertex type `α : Type*`, a subset `W : Set α` of vertices to split, and a proof `hW : W ⊆ G.V` that those vertices are output nodes (the LN's "$W \subseteq V$"). It produces a fresh `CDMG (α ⊕ ↑W)` over a strictly larger carrier. The dot-projection `G.nodeSplittingOn W hW` reads as "the node-splitting of $G$ on $W$" and is the Lean spelling of the LN's subscript $G_{\spl(W)}$.

The crucial Mathlib idiom is the disjoint-sum type `α ⊕ ↑W`. Here `↑W` is the subtype `{x : α // x ∈ W}` — its inhabitants pair a vertex with a proof it lies in `W`. The two constructors `Sum.inl : α → α ⊕ ↑W` and `Sum.inr : ↑W → α ⊕ ↑W` have disjoint ranges, which captures the LN's "$w^0 \neq w^1$" by *type* rather than by hypothesis. We adopt the convention $w^0 := \,$`Sum.inl w` (the *canonical observation copy*, also covering the unsplit vertices $v \notin W$ via $v^0 := v$) and $w^1 := \,$`Sum.inr ⟨w, hw⟩` (the fresh intervention-input label).

A helper `split1 W : α → α ⊕ ↑W` realises the LN's $v \mapsto v^1$ operation via a dependent `if h : v ∈ W` (`dite`): if `v ∈ W` it returns `Sum.inr ⟨v, h⟩`, else `Sum.inl v` (matching the LN's $v^1 := v$ off `W`). Because `v ∈ W` is a `Prop`, the case-split needs a `Decidable` instance; rather than thread one through every call site we discharge it via `Classical.propDecidable` (using `open Classical`) and mark `split1` `noncomputable`. Two `@[simp]` lemmas `split1_of_mem` / `split1_of_not_mem` reduce away the case-split at use sites.

The main definition bundles four data fields, mirroring the LN's four bullets: `J := Sum.inl '' G.J` (the inputs lifted along `inl`); `V := Sum.inl '' G.V ∪ Set.range Sum.inr` (the 0-copies — which, under $W \subseteq V$, contain the LN's $W^0$ — together with the fresh 1-copies $W^1$); `E` as a binary union of the relabelled original edges `(split1 W v₁, Sum.inl v₂)` (source dispatches via `split1`, target via `inl`) and the fresh split edges `(Sum.inl w, Sum.inr w)` for $w \in W$; and `L := (fun (v₁, v₂) ↦ (Sum.inl v₁, Sum.inl v₂)) '' G.L` (both endpoints lifted via `inl`, no case-split needed). Set images use Mathlib's `f '' S` notation, `Set.range f` for the image of all of `f`'s domain.

The seven CDMG structural obligations (`disjoint_JV`, `E_subset`, `L_subset`, `L_irrefl`, `L_symm`, `disjoint_EL`) are discharged inline within the `where` block — each one a short case-split on which piece of the relevant union a hypothetical witness inhabits, then `Sum`-constructor injectivity/disjointness plus the corresponding `G.*` field.
design_choices:
**Carrier type `α ⊕ ↑W`.** Unlike hard intervention (def 3.10), which keeps the original vertex type, node-splitting introduces *fresh* labels (the 1-copies $W^1$), so the result must live over a strictly larger type. We weighed three carriers: `α ⊕ ↑W ⊕ ↑W` (two fresh copies of $W$ — most literal, but ghosts the original `W` out of the result and doubles carrier-shifting in every downstream proof); `α ⊕ α` (awkward and wasteful — forces an explicit identification on $J \cup (V \setminus W)$); and `α ⊕ ↑W`, with the original `α` continuing to host $J$, $V \setminus W$, and the 0-copies via `Sum.inl`. We chose the last: smallest carrier, no quotient, and the LN's "$v^0 := v^1 := v$ for $v \notin W$" becomes the *canonical* `Sum.inl` embedding rather than a side condition. The LN's "we consider $w^0 \neq w^1$" is captured by distinct `Sum` constructors.

**Direction `Sum.inl = 0-copy` (canonical observation copy), `Sum.inr = 1-copy` (fresh intervention-input label).** The LN gives two explicit hints that the 0-copy is the canonical one: a commented-out aside in def 3.11 ("which could be identified with $V$ again if we want to make the identification $W = W^0$"), and the parallel hint in def 3.12 about $W = W^o$. This matches the Richardson–Robins SWIG convention `X = X^o` used throughout the counterfactuals literature. Going the other way would force every downstream SWIG / counterfactual statement to mentally invert the convention.

**`split1` as a standalone, `noncomputable` helper.** The 1-copy operation $v \mapsto v^1$ recurs at the source of every original directed edge and at every later split-related proof (claim 3.6's topological order, claim 3.7's commutativity, claim 3.8's HI/NS commutation, def 3.12's SWIG). Factoring it out under a single name with the two `@[simp]` reduction lemmas lets `simp` discharge the membership case-split once per call site, instead of forcing every downstream proof to re-derive the same `dite`. Since the LN takes `W : Set V` with no decidability hypothesis, `v ∈ W` is a `Prop`; rather than thread a `[Decidable (v ∈ W)]` instance through every call site we lift via `Classical.propDecidable` and mark `split1` `noncomputable`. The construction is `Prop`-valued, so the classical choice has no observable cost.

**No companion `split0` helper.** Under the `Sum.inl = 0-copy` convention, the LN's $v \mapsto v^0$ operation is just the canonical `Sum.inl` embedding — *no* case-split on `v ∈ W` is needed. A hypothetical `split0 W v` would be a no-op alias for `Sum.inl v`. This asymmetry is the entire payoff of fixing the identification direction: only the 1-copy needs a `dite`-style helper.

**Precondition `hW : W ⊆ G.V` is load-bearing.** Hard intervention drops the LN's analogous precondition because the construction stays well-defined for any `W`. Here, the precondition is structurally required: the fresh split edge `(Sum.inl w, Sum.inr ⟨w, _⟩)` needs its source `Sum.inl w` to live in $V_{\spl(W)} = \,$`Sum.inl '' G.V ∪ Set.range Sum.inr`, which only holds when `w ∈ G.V`. Without `W ⊆ G.V`, for $w \in W \setminus G.V$ the split edge would violate `E_subset`. Adding `hW : W ⊆ G.V` matches the LN's "let $W \subseteq V$" exactly.
